title: Phase 0 — Foundation: repo bootstrap, auth, DB, worker, deploy
description:
Bootstrap the Ovyro monorepo from an empty repo. Scope (spec §13 Phase 0 + §8): Next.js 15 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui; Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL with the FULL data model from spec §6 (users, user_roles, sessions, oauth_accounts, seller_onboarding, seller_profiles, listings, listing_media, lists, list_items, leads, meta_connections, meta_event_log, analytics_events) and checked-in migrations in /drizzle; Better Auth with email/password + email OTP + sessions + ADDITIVE roles (user_roles join table, never a single enum — seller superset of buyer); Redis + BullMQ worker service at /worker sharing /src/lib; Resend email; Zod; /api/health endpoint; Sentry; seed script creating an admin user (/scripts); Railway deploy config for web + worker with drizzle-kit migrate as pre-deploy; staging env.

Acceptance: repo builds and typechecks clean; migrations apply to a fresh Postgres; a seeded admin can log in; /api/health returns 200; worker process boots and drains a test job; role helpers enforce additive roles.

Repo structure must match spec §8.2. This is the hard prerequisite for every other phase — no other work can start until this merges.
closeReason: Phase 0 complete: scaffold, worker+queue, schema+auth all merged; 8/8 acceptance criteria verified end-to-end against real Postgres 16 and Redis

title: Phase 1 — Listings core: CRUD wizard, R2 media, public browse + detail SSR, moderation
description:
Spec §13 Phase 1. Listing CRUD wizard in seller dashboard shell; Cloudflare R2 presigned direct photo uploads + sharp variant processing in the worker; public browse/search page and listing detail SSR/ISR page at a clean slug with SEO/OG tags (PUBLIC — no login required, required for Meta ads); admin moderation queue with approve/reject emails; listing status machine (draft/pending_review/active/paused/sold/rejected/expired) and the expiry job.

Acceptance: seller creates a listing with >=1 photo -> enters pending_review -> admin approves -> publicly reachable at clean slug with correct OG tags. Depends on Phase 0.
closeReason: Phase 1 complete: listings-core, seller CRUD+wizard, R2 media pipeline, public browse+SSR detail with OG, admin moderation+emails+expiry — all merged and verified end-to-end (182 tests, lifecycle 31/31 on real Postgres). R2 upload round-trip pending prod creds.

title: Phase 2 — Buyer core: phone OTP, inquiry/lead pipeline, lists, buyer profile
description:
Spec §13 Phase 2. Phone OTP verification (Twilio Verify); inquiry form -> lead creation with rate limiting (Redis sliding window) + captcha; seller lead notification email via worker; seller lead inbox with status pipeline (new/contacted/negotiating/won/lost); buyer 'My Inquiries'; lists/wishlist system (default + custom lists, save button, price_at_save); buyer profile.

Acceptance: buyer registers, verifies phone, browses, filters, saves to a custom list, submits an inquiry with offer amount; lead appears in seller inbox AND arrives by email within 60s AND appears in buyer's My Inquiries. Rate limits block a 20-inquiry burst. Depends on Phase 1.
closeReason: Phase 2 complete: leads-core, phone OTP, inquiry/lead pipeline, seller inbox, buyer account (My Inquiries + lists + profile) — merged and verified end-to-end. Marketplace now generates leads.

title: Phase 3 — Seller onboarding & role model polish
description:
Spec §13 Phase 3. Buyer->seller onboarding state machine (seller_onboarding: in_progress/submitted/approved/rejected) + role switcher UI; seller profile settings; self-inquiry guard (sellers cannot inquire on own listings); per-listing stats.

Acceptance: seller signup grants buyer view automatically; buyer completes onboarding and gains seller dashboard; role switcher works; seller cannot inquire on own listing. Depends on Phase 2.
closeReason: Phase 3 complete: buyer-to-seller onboarding state machine, admin review + additive seller-role grant, role switcher UI, seller profile settings, per-listing stats — merged and verified end-to-end (453 tests, real-server boot, onboarding lifecycle). Self-inquiry guard (Phase 2) regression-verified.

title: Phase 4 — Meta integration: OAuth, pixel injection, CAPI, token health
description:
Spec §5 + §13 Phase 4. FEATURE-FLAGGED. Meta OAuth connect flow; ad account + pixel selection; per-seller pixel injection component with strict isolation tests (a seller's pixel fires ONLY on their own listing pages); PageView/ViewContent/Lead events; CAPI dispatch queue in the worker with event_id dedup (leads.meta_event_id); token health job + needs_reauth flow + seller email; test-event tool; deauth + data-deletion webhooks; cookie-consent gating. Tokens encrypted at rest with TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY, never logged.

Acceptance: connected seller's pixel and only theirs fires on their listing page; test inquiry produces a deduplicated Lead event in Meta Events Manager (pixel + CAPI, same event_id); disconnect stops injection; expired token flips to needs_reauth and emails seller. Depends on Phase 2.
closeReason: Phase 4 (rescoped) complete: seller pastes their full Meta Pixel snippet, server extracts the numeric id (digits-only, raw snippet never stored/injected), single MetaPixel component fires only the listing-owner pixel after cookie consent (PageView/ViewContent + Lead on inquiry with shared event_id). R-4 isolation enforced + tested. No OAuth/CAPI/token/webhooks/flag per user scope. 491 tests green. Merged via OVYRO-8fc2 + OVYRO-850f.

title: Phase 5 — Admin analytics & ops
description:
Spec §10 + §13 Phase 5. analytics_events instrumentation; nightly rollup jobs in the worker; admin overview with charts + users/listings/leads tables; audit log; feature-flag admin UI.

Acceptance: admin dashboard shows buyer count, seller count, Meta-connected seller count (with healthy split), active listings, leads in period, and views->leads funnel — and the numbers reconcile with raw tables. Suspended user cannot log in. Depends on Phase 4. Completes the v1 launch bar (P0-P5).

title: Phase 6 — Video + polish (may trail v1)
description:
Spec §13 Phase 6. Mux video pipeline (upload, processing, playback in listing_media); weekly seller digest email; map-radius search via PostGIS; AddToWishlist Meta events; performance pass.

Explicitly allowed to trail the v1 launch — v1 = Phases 0-5. Depends on Phase 5.

title: Phase 0.2: Data layer + auth — full §6 Drizzle schema, migrations, Better Auth, additive roles, seed
description:
Wave 2a of OVYRO-34b8. Branch from main (scaffold already merged: Next.js 15, TS strict, all deps installed).

SCOPE (yours exclusively): src/lib/db/**, src/lib/auth/**, /drizzle/**, drizzle.config.ts, scripts/seed.ts, src/app/api/auth/**, src/app/(auth)/** (replace placeholders with real forms), src/lib/analytics stub.
DO NOT TOUCH: src/lib/queue, src/lib/email, /worker (builder-worker-queue owns those), or restructure package.json.

1. Full spec §6 schema in Drizzle: users, user_roles, sessions, oauth_accounts, seller_onboarding, seller_profiles, listings, listing_media, lists, list_items, leads, meta_connections, meta_event_log, analytics_events (+ audit_log, feature_flags, listing_reports). ULID text PKs, created_at/updated_at timestamptz everywhere, soft-delete deleted_at where §6 notes it. All §6 indexes including GIN full-text on listings(title||description||address_text).
2. Checked-in migrations in /drizzle via drizzle-kit generate. Additive/backwards-compatible only.
3. Better Auth: email/password + email OTP + sessions. Postgres via pg (already pinned 8.22.0).
4. ROLES ARE ADDITIVE — user_roles join table, NEVER a role enum column on users. seller superset of buyer. Role helpers (hasRole/requireRole) with unit tests proving a seller passes buyer checks. Buyer-facing checks must be is-authenticated, never role==='buyer'.
5. scripts/seed.ts: idempotent, creates admin user (admin role granted here only — never self-serve).

ACCEPTANCE: bun run build/typecheck/lint/test all green; migrations apply cleanly to a fresh Postgres; seeded admin can log in; role helper tests prove additive behavior. tsconfig include is broad so /scripts typechecks — write it typesafe from commit 1. Zod is v4 (.error/.issues differs from v3). Do not bump next/react/typescript/eslint/tailwind — see pinned versions in the scaffold handoff.
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-data-auth/OVYRO-ba7c (29 files; typecheck/lint clean, 32 pass 0 fail; additive user_roles verified; scope clean)

title: Phase 0.3: Worker service — BullMQ + Redis, Resend email, /worker processors
description:
Wave 2b of OVYRO-34b8. Branch from main (scaffold already merged: Next.js 15, TS strict, all deps installed incl. bullmq 5.80.10, ioredis 5.11.1, resend 6.18.0).

SCOPE (yours exclusively): src/lib/queue/**, src/lib/email/**, /worker/**, src/lib/r2 stub.
DO NOT TOUCH: src/lib/db, src/lib/auth, /drizzle, drizzle.config.ts, scripts/seed.ts (builder-data-auth owns those), or restructure package.json.

1. src/lib/queue: BullMQ queue definitions + shared ioredis connection (env REDIS_URL). Typed job payloads via Zod (v4). Define queue names for the async work spec §8 calls for: email, meta-capi, media-processing, token-health, listing-expiry — processors for the latter arrive in later phases, but the queue/registry shape lands now.
2. src/lib/email: Resend client + a sendEmail wrapper. Env-guarded so it no-ops without RESEND_API_KEY (tests must not hit the network).
3. /worker: standalone entrypoint (bun run worker -> worker/index.ts, script slot already exists). Boots, connects Redis, registers processors, graceful SIGTERM shutdown. This is a SEPARATE Railway service — it must NOT import Next.js server runtime. It shares /src/lib per spec §8.2.
4. A test job type that the worker drains end-to-end, proving enqueue -> process -> complete.
5. src/lib/r2: thin stub for Cloudflare R2 presigned uploads (Phase 1 fills it in) — types + interface only, no heavy impl.

CRITICAL (CLAUDE.md): the web app stays STATELESS. All async work runs here, never in the web process.

ACCEPTANCE: bun run build/typecheck/lint/test all green; worker boots and drains the test job against a local Redis; worker process does not pull in Next.js runtime. tsconfig include is broad so /worker typechecks automatically — write it typesafe from commit 1. Do not bump next/react/typescript/eslint/tailwind — see pinned versions in the scaffold handoff.
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-worker-queue/OVYRO-5983 (15 files, 21/21 tests, runtime-verified drain)

title: Phase 0.4: Integration verification — deps, migrations on real Postgres, seed admin, prove P0 acceptance end-to-end
description:
Final Phase 0 gate. All three wave branches are merged to main (scaffold, worker+queue, db+auth). Each was verified only inside its own worktree; NOTHING has yet verified the integrated stack. Branch from main.

CONTEXT: the canonical root has no node_modules — dependency installation was never run there — so tsc/eslint are missing and 4 tests fail on unresolved packages. That looks like an environment gap, not a code defect. Confirm that is all it is.

DO:
1. Install dependencies at the repo root (bun). Verify the pinned versions did NOT drift: next 15.5.21, react 19.2.8, typescript 5.9.3, eslint 9.39.5, eslint-config-next 15.5.21, tailwindcss 4.3.3. Unpinned resolution previously pulled typescript@7 / eslint@10 / eslint-config-next@16, which peer-conflict with Next 15. If bun.lock drifts, restore the pinned line and say so.
2. Full gates green on main: build, typecheck, lint, test.
3. REAL POSTGRES. If none runs locally, start one (Docker preferred, Homebrew acceptable). Apply /drizzle migrations to a FRESH database via the db:migrate script. Confirm every spec section-6 table and index materializes — especially the GIN full-text index on listings. Report the actual table list you observed, not the list you expected.
4. Run the seed script against that database. Confirm it creates the admin user, is IDEMPOTENT (run twice; second run must not error or duplicate), and that admin is granted through the user_roles join table — never a column on users.
5. Prove ADDITIVE ROLES against real data: create a seller, confirm it carries BOTH seller and buyer rows and passes a buyer-facing check. This is the domain rule most likely to be silently broken.
6. Prove login works end-to-end for the seeded admin (Better Auth against the real DB), not merely that the route compiles.
7. Worker: with real Redis, boot the worker, enqueue a job from a SEPARATE process, confirm drain, confirm graceful SIGTERM.
8. /api/health returns 200 at real runtime (start the server and curl it).

REPORT HONESTLY: if a criterion cannot be met, say which and why — do not paper over it. If you install services on this machine (Postgres/Redis), state exactly what you installed and whether you left it running; the user needs to know what changed on their machine.

FIX-FORWARD: you may fix small integration defects you find (a missing var in .env.example, migration ordering, EMAIL_FROM which the worker builder flagged as unadded). Anything larger, report rather than silently rewriting another builder's design.

ACCEPTANCE: every item above green, or explicitly reported as blocked with the reason.
closeReason: Merged; all 8 P0 acceptance criteria verified green against real Postgres 16 + Redis

title: Phase 1.0: Listings core — shared status machine, Zod schemas, slug util, DTO types
description:
Wave 1 of Phase 1 (OVYRO-133c). The SHARED foundation that three wave-2 feature builders (seller CRUD, public browse, admin moderation) all depend on. Land it first so they do not each redefine the status machine and listing types differently. Branch from main. Phase 0 is merged and deployed: full spec-6 Drizzle schema (listings and listing_media already exist), Better Auth with additive roles, worker plus queue.

SCOPE (yours exclusively): src/lib/listings/. Do NOT create API routes, pages, or worker processors. Do NOT touch src/lib/db schema (it exists), auth, queue, or restructure package.json.

BUILD:
1. src/lib/listings/status.ts. The listing status state machine from spec section 4.3.1 Figure 3. Statuses: draft, pending_review, active, paused, sold, rejected, expired. Encode the ALLOWED transitions exactly (word TO): draft TO pending_review (submit); pending_review TO active (admin approve); pending_review TO rejected (admin reject); active TO paused and active TO sold; paused TO active (reactivate); active TO expired (90 days idle); expired TO pending_review (renew via edit and resubmit); rejected TO pending_review (edit and resubmit). Export canTransition(from,to) guard plus assertTransition that throws on illegal moves. Unit tests for legal and illegal transitions.
2. src/lib/listings/schema.ts. Zod (v4) schemas for listing create and edit input covering ALL wizard fields (spec 4.3.1): title, description, land_type enum, price, currency, negotiable, area plus area_unit enum, address_text, city, region, country, lat and lng optional, survey_number optional, zoning optional, road_access and water and electricity optional bools, legal_docs_available. Import and reuse the DB enums from src/lib/db/schema. Do not re-declare drifting copies.
3. src/lib/listings/slug.ts. Slug generation from title (kebab, ascii-fold, deduped) plus a uniqueness helper that queries listings.slug and appends a suffix on collision.
4. src/lib/listings/types.ts. Shared ListingDTO and ListingSummary types used by both public and dashboard serialization, plus serialize() from the Drizzle row.
5. src/lib/listings/index.ts. Barrel re-export.

ACCEPTANCE: build, typecheck, lint, test all green; status tests prove illegal transitions rejected; Zod reuses DB enums (no drift). Keep it small and dependency-light. Do not bump next, react, typescript, eslint, tailwind. Zod is v4.
closeReason: Merged; gates green (69 pass), enums reused from DB schema (no drift), status machine exhaustively tested, scope clean

Out of scope: leads P2, stats P3, Meta P4, video P6. Acceptance: gates green; draft to pending_review only with 1 plus photos; ownership and status guards enforced. Do not bump pinned deps; Zod v4.

title: Phase 1.2: R2 media pipeline — presign, sharp variants, blurhash, reorder
description: Wave 2 of Phase 1 (OVYRO-133c). Branch from main AFTER OVYRO-1042 (listings-core) merges. Read spec sections 4.3.1 media rules and 7. PHOTOS ONLY — video/Mux is Phase 6; you may register kind=photo paths and leave video untouched. SCOPE (yours only): src/app/api/dashboard/media/, src/lib/r2/ (fill in the Phase 0 stub), worker/processors/media-processing.ts (register it in the worker processor map), and a media-uploader component under src/components/dashboard/media/. Do NOT touch src/lib/listings, listing CRUD routes, public or admin pages, or restructure package.json. Build: seller-gated POST media/presign returning a presigned R2 PUT url (direct-to-storage, never proxy bytes through the app — Railway must not buffer large files); POST media/complete that registers a listing_media row (processing_status uploading) and enqueues a media-processing job; worker processor downloads from R2, uses sharp to make responsive webp variants plus a blurhash placeholder, writes them back to R2, flips processing_status to ready (or failed); ownership checks so a seller only attaches media to their own listing; drag-to-reorder endpoint updating sort_order, first photo is cover. Constraints: jpg/png/webp/heic, 15MB max each, up to 25 photos per listing. Secrets from env (R2_*). Acceptance: gates green; presign plus complete plus worker processing produces ready webp variants and a blurhash for a real uploaded photo; oversize/wrong-type rejected; reorder persists. Do not bump pinned deps; Zod v4.
closeReason: Merged; 100 tests green, presign no byte-proxy, sharp+blurhash+R2, ownership enforced, deps added cleanly

title: Phase 1.3: Public browse/search plus listing detail SSR with SEO/OG
description: Wave 2 of Phase 1 (OVYRO-133c). Branch from main AFTER OVYRO-1042 (listings-core) merges; import src/lib/listings for types and serialize. Read spec sections 4.2.1 and 7. These pages are PUBLIC (no login — required for Meta ads) and must be SSR/ISR, fast, and SEO-friendly. SCOPE (yours only): src/app/(public)/ (home, /land, /land/[slug]), src/app/api/listings/, src/lib/search/. Do NOT touch dashboard, admin, media endpoints, worker, or src/lib/listings internals. Build: GET /api/listings with filters (location text, region, price range, area range with unit, land_type, road_access, utilities), sort (newest, price, area, popularity), and CURSOR pagination; Postgres full-text search on title/description/address_text (the GIN index listings_fts_idx exists from Phase 0); only status=active, non-deleted listings are public. GET /api/listings/[slug] for detail. Browse grid/list at / and /land with the filter UI. Listing detail at /land/[slug] = the seller landing page: gallery (photos; leave a video slot for P6), full description, parcel details, price and negotiable flag, seller display name. MUST emit correct OpenGraph/Twitter meta tags and canonical URL via Next metadata (uses NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL). Increment listings.view_count on detail view (denormalized counter). Leave a labeled mount point for the inquiry form and save button but DO NOT build them (leads and lists are Phase 2). Acceptance: gates green; public browse filters and full-text search work against seeded active listings; detail page renders server-side with correct OG tags at a clean slug and is reachable without login; drafts/pending/rejected never appear publicly. Do not bump pinned deps; Zod v4.
closeReason: Merged; 108 tests green, public visibility invariant (active + non-deleted) enforced at base predicate with test, SSR/OG, scope clean

title: Phase 1.4: Admin moderation queue, approve/reject emails, expiry job
description: Wave 2 of Phase 1 (OVYRO-133c). Branch from main AFTER OVYRO-1042 (listings-core) merges; drive all status changes through assertTransition from core. Read spec sections 4.1 and 4.3.1 and 7. SCOPE (yours only): src/app/admin/ (moderation UI), src/app/api/admin/listings/, worker/processors/listing-expiry.ts (register in worker map), and admin moderation email templates under src/emails/ or src/lib/email/templates. Do NOT touch dashboard, public pages, media endpoints, src/lib/listings internals, or restructure package.json. All routes admin-gated via Phase 0 requireRole admin. Build: moderation queue listing all pending_review listings at /admin/listings (or /admin/moderation); approve action (pending_review to active, set published_at, set expires_at to 90 days out) and reject action (pending_review to rejected with rejected_reason); both send the seller an email via the worker email queue (approved: your listing is live at slug; rejected: reason). Write an admin_audit_log row for each moderation action. listing-expiry worker processor: a scheduled/repeatable job that flips active listings past expires_at to expired and (optionally) emails a renew prompt. Acceptance: gates green; admin approves a pending listing making it public and emailing the seller; admin rejects with a reason emailing the seller; audit log rows written; expiry job flips an overdue active listing to expired. Non-admins get 403. Do not bump pinned deps; Zod v4.
closeReason: Merged (ai-resolve on worker/processors/index.ts, verified both processors retained); 101 tests green, admin-gated, audit log written, status via assertTransition

title: Phase 1.5: Integration verification — full gates on merged main, listing lifecycle end-to-end
description: Final Phase 1 gate. All five wave branches (listings-core, seller-crud, media, public-browse, admin-moderation) are merged to main; each was verified only in its own worktree and media+admin-mod had an AI-resolved conflict on worker/processors/index.ts. Branch from main. Read spec sections 4.2, 4.3.1, 4.1. DO: 1) Install deps at repo root (bun); media added sharp, blurhash, @aws-sdk/client-s3, @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner — confirm bun.lock is consistent and the Phase 0 pins did not drift (next 15.5.21, react 19.2.8, typescript 5.9.3, eslint 9.39.5, tailwindcss 4.3.3). 2) Full gates green on merged main: build, typecheck, lint, test. The Next build must compile all new routes and pages together (this is the real cross-module check — seller wizard mounting the media uploader, admin approve calling the email queue). 3) Real Postgres: migrations still apply cleanly to a fresh DB (schema unchanged in P1 but confirm); seed admin. 4) Verify the worker/processors registry has ALL FOUR processors after the AI merge: email, system, media-processing, listing-expiry — boot the worker and confirm it registers them without error. 5) Listing lifecycle end-to-end against the real DB and HTTP routes: seed a seller; create a draft listing via the dashboard API; confirm submit is blocked with zero photos; confirm ownership (a second seller gets 404 on the first sellers listing); admin approves (pending_review to active, published_at + expires_at set, audit_log row written); confirm the listing is then reachable on the public detail route at its slug with correct OG tags and does NOT appear while draft/pending/rejected; run the public browse API with a filter and full-text search and confirm only active listings return. 6) MEDIA CAVEAT: no R2 credentials exist locally or in prod. Test presign request validation, ownership checks, size/type limits, and the media-processing worker logic as far as possible WITHOUT real R2; explicitly report what is blocked on missing R2_* credentials rather than faking it. REPORT HONESTLY which criteria pass, which are blocked and why. Fix-forward only small integration defects (a bad import between the merged modules, a missing env var); anything larger, report. State any services you start on this machine and whether you left them running. ACCEPTANCE: every item green or explicitly reported blocked with reason.
closeReason: Merged; all 6 criteria green: 182 tests/0 fail, next build 15/15 pages, lifecycle E2E 31/31 on real Postgres, 4 worker processors; fixed stale queue-registry test; R2/Resend round-trip blocked on missing creds (expected)

title: Local dev bring-up plus demo seed — populated marketplace at localhost:3000 without R2/Twilio/Resend
description: Goal: let the human run the platform locally, see a POPULATED marketplace, iterate, before we push to GitHub. Branch from main.
